seoTitle: Create an Event Calendar in Confluence (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to create an event calendar in Confluence in 2026 using Team Calendars, add milestones and event types, sync Jira releases, and embed it live.
intro: Chasing launch dates through scattered spreadsheets and email threads wastes time and creates confusion. This guide shows how to set up a shared event calendar in Confluence, so milestones, releases, and team leave are all visible in one central place.

steps.heading: Open Team Calendars
steps.body: Navigate to the left sidebar menu, click Calendars, then click Add Calendar.

steps.heading: Create your calendar
steps.body: Select Create New Calendar, give it a descriptive title, and map it to your shared space.

steps.heading: Add events
steps.body: Click Add Event to build individual milestones with start times, summaries, and reminders.

steps.heading: Set event types and colors
steps.body: Open Settings to add event types, assign distinct colors, and manage team permissions.

steps.heading: Sync with Jira (optional)
steps.body: Click Add Calendar and select Jira Software to automatically track live epic dates and releases.

steps.heading: Embed it on a page
steps.body: Go to your space homepage, type `/calendar`, and embed the interactive display panel.

faq.question: Can I sync Jira release dates into my Confluence calendar?
faq.answer: Yes, choosing the Jira Software option when adding a calendar pulls live epic dates and releases automatically.

faq.question: Can I import an external calendar like Google Calendar or Outlook?
faq.answer: Yes, you can pass secure iCal tracking links from Outlook or Google Calendar to display outside itineraries.

faq.question: Can different event types have different colors?
faq.answer: Yes, the calendar settings let you build distinct tracking colors for milestones, travel, releases, and team leave.

faq.question: Can I embed the calendar on my team's homepage?
faq.answer: Yes, typing `/calendar` on the space homepage lets you embed the interactive event grid there.
